message:
fix(core): fail-closed vendored compatibility, tailer streaming gate, and telemetry blindness detection (GH #44)

vendored.rs: Flip three "assume compatible" fallback paths to
"refuse vendored" — missing vendored commit metadata, missing local
WezTerm version, and unparseable commit hash now all produce
Incompatible with allow_vendored: false.  Previously the vendored
mux backend would optimistically connect when version info was
incomplete, risking protocol corruption.  Now it defensively refuses,
falling back to polling mode.  Renames wa -> ft in recommendation
strings.

tailer.rs: Gate streaming mode on compatibility_report().  Previously
detect_tailer_mode() only checked whether a mux socket path existed.
Now it calls read_local_wezterm_version() and compatibility_report()
and only allows Streaming if allow_vendored is true.  Extracted
streaming_socket_configured() and detect_tailer_mode_with_allow_vendored()
helpers.  Tests rewritten for determinism.

wezterm.rs: Convert two silent Ok(None) returns in
get_pane_tiered_scrollback_status() to explicit errors — when vendored
mux returns no tiered scrollback status, and when the circuit breaker
is open with no CLI fallback.  These were invisible data loss paths.

runtime.rs: PaneTieredScrollbackFetch gains error_samples (up to 4
error messages), telemetry_blind() (all panes errored, zero summaries),
and telemetry_partial() (some panes succeeded, others errored).
Structured warn!() log lines emitted when blind/partial, and
maintenance record metadata enriched with tiered_scrollback_telemetry_
blind, telemetry_partial, error_samples, and blind_reason fields.
Two unit tests validate blind/partial detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
